_about: The §3 adversarial taxonomy as DATA so coverage is enumerable, not prose. The DESIGN phase draws from this. Two passes: a breadth sweep (these classes) + a targeted business-logic reasoning pass. surface_class values match the schema enum.

boundary.note: boundary value analysis — attack every boundary at -1 / on / +1

signed_fractional: the classic: negative quantity yields a credit

unicode.note: high-yield for this repo's CJK context

sequence.note: business-logic core — scanners can't find these
values: calls out of intended order (1→3 skipping 2)
privileged/admin call from a normal session
retry storm (30% packet loss → 4x retry amplification, no backoff)

resource.note: chaos fault catalog, smallest scope first

integration.note: where mocks hide bugs — attack with REAL collaborators

stride_checklist._about: STRIDE-per-component breadth checklist so no abuse category is skipped
stride_checklist.elevation: do what the current state/role forbids

idea_attack_catalog._about: v0.3.0 IDEA mode (target.type 'idea'): the reasoning-attack families DESIGN draws from when red-teaming an argument/design/plan (debate con-side). The unifying move is still spec-inversion: invert the claim into its violations. Maps to the idea oracles in references/oracle-menu.md (§I1–§I6).
idea_attack_catalog.counterexample: one concrete case inside the claim's own domain where the claimed outcome does NOT hold (a single real case falls a universal 'every X' claim)
idea_attack_catalog.contradiction: the claim's premises (or two stated commitments) cannot both hold — self-defeating
idea_attack_catalog.unmet_assumption: a load-bearing premise the conclusion depends on is false / unsupported in the stated context
idea_attack_catalog.scope_violation: the claim over-reaches its evidence/domain — supported only in a narrower scope than asserted (over-generalization)
idea_attack_catalog.infeasibility: sound in principle but impossible / too costly within the declared constraints (resources, time, dependencies, physics)
idea_attack_catalog.missing_case: an unhandled case the claim/plan was obligated to cover (by its own scope or success criteria) but does not — a coverage gap
idea_attack_catalog.ambiguity_equivocation: a key term shifts meaning between premises, so the conclusion only 'follows' by trading on the slippage
idea_attack_catalog.false_dichotomy_or_hidden_alternative: the argument assumes only the options it lists; exhibit a real third option it ignored

idea_anti_vacuity_rule: A vague 'I disagree' with no concrete counter (no counterexample / named premise / binding constraint) is NOT a finding. The critique must attack the actual steelmanned claim (not_strawman) and demonstrate observed != expected (the counter outcome != what the claim predicts).
anti_vacuity_rule: A malformed input the contract NEVER promised to handle, correctly rejected, is NOT a finding. The oracle violation must be a property the feature actually promised.
